contents.summary: As a Main Admin, I want Ask AI (Alpha) access restricted to Main Admins behind a feature flag so only authorized users can use the feature
contents.description:
As a Main Admin / I want the Ask AI (Alpha) feature to be visible and accessible only to Main Admins and controlled by a feature flag / So that we can safely rollout and test Ask AI in alpha without exposing it to other users.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Backend: a role-based permission check exists for Ask AI that returns allowed=true only for Main Admins.
- Feature flag: a feature-flag toggle exists (e.g., feature.ask_ai_alpha) that can enable/disable the feature per tenant.
- UI: the AI Assist button is only rendered when both the feature flag is enabled and the user has Main Admin role.
- Automated tests: unit/integration tests validate that non-Main-Admin users do not see the button and Main Admins do.
- Documentation: API or permission contract is documented for other teams to integrate.

contents.summary: As a user, I want a 'Limit to current view' checkbox for Ask AI that defaults to on and can be tested independently
contents.description:
As a user / I want a 'Limit to current view' checkbox below the question field that defaults to enabled and can be toggled / So that I can control whether AI searches only the current location or the whole platform.

Acceptance Criteria:
- UI: the checkbox is present, enabled by default when the panel opens.
- Behavior: the checkbox state is emitted as part of the Ask request payload and respected by the mocked AI service during development.
- Integration: the control works when Ask AI is hidden behind the feature flag (it should not be visible to non-Main-Admins but can be validated in dev when feature flag is enabled).
- Tests: unit/UX tests covering default state and toggle behavior.

contents.summary: As a user, I want an Ask button that triggers the AI search (UI + mock backend) so I can test interaction independent of production AI
contents.description:
As a user / I want an Ask button under the question field that is disabled by default and triggers a search when enabled (click or Enter) / So that I can submit questions to the AI search during alpha testing.

Acceptance Criteria:
- UI: Ask button present under question field and disabled when panel opens.
- Enablement: entering a valid question enables the Ask button.
- Interaction: clicking the Ask button or pressing Enter sends the request to the AI endpoint (initially a mocked endpoint that follows the agreed contract) and displays the mocked response.
- Tooltip: when enabled, hover text shows 'Click or hit enter to ask your question.'
- Visibility: button is only visible when feature flag and permission allow (ties to IB2-318 implementation).

contents.summary: As a Main Admin, I want the Ask button to execute searches using the 'Limit to current view' state and mocks so the end-to-end interaction can be validated
contents.description:
As a Main Admin / I want the Ask button to use the Limit-to-current-view checkbox state when executing searches and to work against a mocked AI backend / So that the full Ask flow can be validated in alpha without the production backend.

Acceptance Criteria:
- The Ask button only appears when Ask AI Alpha feature flag and Main Admin permission are present (ties to IB2-318 slice).
- When clicked, the request payload includes the limitToCurrentView boolean and the question text.
- The mocked AI backend returns deterministic responses for both 'limited' and 'global' scope allowing QA to validate UI differences.
- Keyboard Enter triggers same behavior as button click.
- Tests for the combined interaction are included.

contents.summary: As a Public user, I want the guest upload page header simplified (no DB name, no close icon) so public flows have a minimal header
contents.description:
As a Public user / I want the public Create Record page header to omit the Database name and hide the close ('x') icon / So that public upload pages use a simplified header before applying randomized success styles.

Acceptance Criteria:
- For public (unauthenticated/public-link) upload flows, the page header shows only 'Create {{Record}}'.
- The Database name and 'x' close icon are not rendered in this mode.
- The header change is toggleable behind a rollout config and documented for QA.
- Unit/integration tests confirm header differences between public and authenticated flows.

contents.summary: As a Public User, I want a tasteful but consistent Guest Upload success confirmation (configurable experiment with limited, deterministic variants) so the UX remains trustworthy while enabling visual experiments
contents.description:
In order to run visual experiments without degrading user trust, As a Public User I want the Guest Upload success confirmation to present from a limited, deterministic set of consistent presentation variants (e.g., 3 predetermined layouts) rather than fully random changes. The main header text remains 'Your {{Record}} has been submitted'. Variants are delivered behind a feature flag/experiment with automated visual regression tests and a safe fallback for simplified header absence.

Acceptance Criteria (binary, Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Deterministic variant selection behind feature flag
Given the feature-flagged visual experiment is enabled
When a user submits a guest upload
Then one of three predefined, tested presentation variants is selected deterministically (seeded by session/experiment id) and shown

Scenario 2 — Header text unchanged
Given any variant is shown
When the success confirmation is displayed
Then the main header text reads exactly 'Your {{Record}} has been submitted'

Scenario 3 — Adapts to header rollout or fallback
Given the simplified header is present or absent
When the success confirmation renders
Then the confirmation adapts to the available layout; if simplified header absent it uses the safe fallback layout (no dependency on header removal)

Scenario 4 — Feature flag and visual tests
Given the experiment is behind a feature flag
When automated visual regression tests run
Then each predefined variant has a golden image test and passes visual diffs

Scenario 5 — Accessibility & consistency
Given the variant is selected
When rendered
Then it meets accessibility contrast and screen-reader requirements and does not change wording or primary layout unpredictably across submissions

contents.summary: As a consumer, I want a Get Smart Pages API contract and mocked endpoint so frontend can integrate immediately
contents.description:
As a developer / I want a documented REST API contract (GET /smart-pages) and a mock implementation that returns a representative list of Smart Pages / So that frontend teams can build and test UI independently of backend production implementation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- API contract (OpenAPI/Swagger fragment) for GET /smart-pages exists and is agreed upon.
- A mocked endpoint that follows the contract is available for frontend integration and CI tests.
- The mock supports pagination, basic filters, and returns stable identifiers usable by detail/delete APIs.
- Tests and example payloads are included in the repo and API docs.

contents.summary: As a consumer, I want Delete Smart Page API implemented against the agreed contract and a mock so it can be tested
contents.description:
As a developer / I want a DELETE /smart-pages/{id} endpoint implemented and testable against the agreed contract (or a mock) / So that UI and integration tests for delete flows can be validated without the final backend.

Acceptance Criteria:
- DELETE /smart-pages/{id} accepts the identifier format defined by the details API.
- Behavior for soft vs hard delete is defined and documented; the endpoint returns appropriate status codes.
- A mock or staging implementation is available to run integration tests.
- API documentation and examples are provided.

contents.summary: As a user, I want a Status column component that shows colored status chips so it can be reused across pages
contents.description:
As a user / I want the Status column to display status chips (Pending, Approved, Declined, Reviewed, Cancelled) with the updated color scheme / So that the status is visible and consistent across the UI.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A reusable StatusChip UI component exists and renders the five statuses with the new colors.
- Component includes accessibility labels and tooltip text explaining the status.
- Component behavior is unit-tested and documented for reuse.
- The component can render a static status or be fed dynamic state from a parent.

contents.summary: As a user, I want to navigate to all Pages and specific Stages on a record with a static nav first so stage-aware features can be built incrementally
contents.description:
As a user / I want to view and navigate to Pages and Stages for Single and Staged Records, with staged-record navigation showing future stages disabled when pending / So that other status and stage-aware UI can rely on a stable navigation contract.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Single record view allows navigation to any Page/Section.
- For staged records in Pending, Stage 1 is shown as active and future stages render as disabled controls.
- Navigation component emits events or route changes that other components (e.g., Request Status) can subscribe to.
- Basic unit/integration tests ensure stage display rules work as specified.

contents.summary: As a user, I want to see the Record Request Status section that displays current status and remaining approvals (uses static/nav hooks initially)
contents.description:
As a user / I want a Request Status section on the Record Publish Approval page that lists Request Status, Approvals Still Required, and Waiting On, and which reflects the currently viewed Stage when stage navigation is present / So that reviewers have the same contextual info as other V3 approval types.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Request Status section shows Request Status, Approvals Still Required, and Waiting On fields.
- When stage navigation (IB2-44) is present, the section can accept a stage context and update displayed status accordingly; otherwise it shows the overall request status.
- The section works with static/mock data and subscribes to nav events for stage changes.
- Tests validate rendering in single-stage and staged contexts.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want an 'Enable Azure Image Tagging' MCP setting so we can switch providers to Azure
contents.description:
As an Admin / I want a new MCP boolean setting under Asset Intelligence named 'Enable Image Tagging (Azure)' that controls whether Azure is used for image tagging and disables Imagga when enabled / So that the system can be toggled to Azure tagger safely.

Acceptance Criteria:
- MCP setting exists in Admin > Asset Intelligence and is persisted per tenant.
- When enabled, the platform will prefer Azure for image tagging; when disabled, existing provider (Imagga) remains active.
- UI config and API reflect the new setting and it is behind a rollout toggle for staged deployment.
- Tests confirm provider selection logic based on the setting.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want an 'Azure Image Processing Limit (Images)' setting so we can quota Azure usage per billing cycle
contents.description:
As an Admin / I want a numeric MCP setting 'Azure Image Processing Limit (Images) (per billing cycle)' that caps Azure image processing usage and integrates with billing/quota checks / So that we can enforce tenant-level limits on Azure consumption.

Acceptance Criteria:
- New numeric setting exists and is validated (non-negative integer).
- Runtime enforcement: image processing requests are denied or queued when the limit is reached and appropriate error codes/messages returned.
- Quota consumption metrics are emitted for billing integration.
- Tests cover enforcement and edge cases.

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want Imagga->Azure image-tagging migration scripts for metadata and usage history (migration MVP) so data continuity is maintained
contents.description:
As an engineer / I want scripts and a measured plan to migrate Imagga image-tagging configurations and usage history to Azure (MVP with sampling and verification) / So that we preserve tag metadata and usage continuity for billing and reporting.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Migration plan, scripts, and verification steps are documented.
- A tested migration job migrates a sample dataset and validates tag counts and metadata integrity.
- Rollback/compensating steps defined.
- Migration is gated behind testing and rollout procedures.

contents.summary: As a product owner, I want basic Azure image-tagging analytics and notifications so we can monitor usage and failures
contents.description:
As a product owner / I want analytics for Azure image-tagging usage (counts per tenant) and notifications for quota exhaustion and provider failures / So that operations and tenants can be alerted and billed correctly.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Usage metrics for Azure image processing (counts per tenant, per billing cycle) are emitted to the analytics pipeline.
- Notification rules exist for quota exhaustion and provider errors; notifications can be sent to admins.
- Dashboards or a basic report demonstrates the metrics.
- Tests and monitoring alerts are in place.

contents.summary: As an analyst, I want the weekly report renamed and to include Q/A/Rating data so report consumers get the new metrics
contents.description:
As an analyst / I want the existing weekly report updated to include Question/Answer/Rating data and to be renamed to 'weekly_asset_intelligence_report' / So that stakeholders receive the consolidated asset-intelligence metrics.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Report schema updated to include Question, Answer, and Rating fields.
- Report file/name renamed to weekly_asset_intelligence_report.
- Report generation includes tests showing new fields present for a sample dataset.

contents.summary: As an analyst, I want dev server entries removed from the weekly report filters so production reports exclude dev noise
contents.description:
As an analyst / I want the report generation pipeline to exclude known dev server entries (e.g., dev018, dev015, etc.) and document the exclusion list / So that weekly reports only include production/staging environments.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Filter list is configurable and documented.
- Report output no longer contains data from excluded dev/test servers in a sample run.
- Unit tests validate filtering logic.

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want a plan and tooling for historical data migration and multi-environment aggregation (phase 1) so historical reporting is accurate
contents.description:
As an engineer / I want an actionable plan and an initial ETL job that supports migrating historical Question/Answer/Rating data and aggregates across environments for reporting (phase 1 MVP) / So that historical reports reflect consolidated metrics.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Migration/ETL plan documented and an initial job implemented for a bounded time-range with verification steps.
- Aggregation logic supports multi-environment keys and preserves tenant isolation.
- Backfill runs for a sample historical range are validated.

contents.summary: As an editor, I want to move a sub-page to become a standalone page via drag-and-drop (core move functionality) so basic restructuring is possible
contents.description:
As an editor / I want the ability in Page Builder to drag a sub-page and promote it to a standalone page (parent) and persist the updated navigation order / So that users can reorganize page hierarchies.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Drag-and-drop operation promotes a sub-page to top-level and updates navigation order.
- Child references remain resolvable (basic redirection scheme) and the navigation persists across editing/viewing.
- Basic automated tests and a migration plan for references are included.
- The operation is permission-guarded and emits an audit event.

contents.summary: As an editor, I want child reference migration and URL/breadcrumb updates when a sub-page becomes standalone so links remain valid
contents.description:
As an editor / I want automatic migration of child references, updates to page URLs, breadcrumbs, and SEO metadata when a sub-page is promoted / So that end-users and search engines still reach the correct content.

Acceptance Criteria:
- References and internal links are updated or redirected to the new URL.
- Breadcrumbs and SEO metadata are updated for the moved page.
- A test suite verifies link integrity for a sample site after move.
- Rollback/redirect strategies are documented.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want to view and configure sub-page permissions inline in Admin->Group so I can manage access at page level
contents.description:
As an Admin / I want to list sub-pages under parent pages in Admin->Group->Permission and set basic permissions inline (read/edit) / So that administrators can manage sub-page access without deep hierarchical complexity.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Admin UI lists pages and sub-pages and allows setting basic permission roles per page.
- Changes persist and are enforced by the application auth layer.
- Tests validate permission assignments and enforcement for common role scenarios.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want permission propagation (single-level) for sub-pages so I can apply permissions to children efficiently
contents.description:
As an Admin / I want to trigger permission propagation from a parent to immediate child sub-pages using the same logic as resource folders (single-level propagation) / So that admins can quickly apply common permissions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A 'Propagate to children' action propagates permissions to direct children and records the change in audit logs.
- Undo of propagation is possible in the same session (MVP undo) or documented rollback.
- Tests validate propagation behavior for simple hierarchies.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want requester email templates to support one additional tenant language variant (MVP) so tenant notifications can be localized
contents.description:
In order to localize requester notifications for tenants without adding full i18n overhead, As an Admin I want the MVP to support a single extra language variant per tenant for requester email templates and a documented extension path for future languages.

Acceptance Criteria (binary, Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Template language variant storage
Given an email template exists
When an Admin adds a second language variant for a tenant
Then the template stores two variants (default + tenant language) and preserves placeholder substitution

Scenario 2 — Selection at send time
Given a request is generated for a tenant with a language setting
When the system constructs the requester email
Then it selects the template variant that matches the tenant language setting (falls back to default if missing)

Scenario 3 — Tests cover multilingual generation
Given sample templates and a tenant with the second language configured
When automated tests run
Then email generation for the sample template uses the tenant language variant and substitutes placeholders correctly

Scenario 4 — Documentation for extension
Given product docs are published
When Admins consult docs
Then a brief extension path is described showing how to add further languages in future releases

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want notification sends to emit basic delivery events (sent, delivered, bounced) to a tracking stream so we can later add real-time tracking and provider sync
contents.description:
In order to enable future real-time delivery tracking and provider synchronization, As an Admin I want the notification system to emit basic delivery events (sent, delivered, bounced) into a tracking table or event stream as an MVP.

Acceptance Criteria (binary, Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Event emission on send
Given a notification send operation completes successfully
When the system sends the notification
Then a 'notification.sent' event is written to the tracking table/stream with tenant, template_id, recipient, timestamp

Scenario 2 — Final status events
Given the notification later receives a delivery status (delivered or bounced)
When the system receives that status from the provider or internal result
Then a corresponding 'notification.delivered' or 'notification.bounced' event is emitted with tenant, template_id, recipient, timestamp, and status

Scenario 3 — Tests validate event content
Given unit/integration tests run
When simulated send and bounce/deliver events are triggered
Then tests assert the tracking records contain the required fields and valid timestamps

Notes: This story is explicitly scoped to event emission only — it does not implement dashboards or external provider sync, which will be separate work items.

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want idempotency-key handling and duplicate detection so clients can safely retry requests (functional correctness; perf validation separate)
contents.description:
In order to allow safe client retries and avoid duplicate processing, As an engineer I want idempotency-key handling and duplicate detection implemented so retries return the original requestId and duplicates are reported during validation. Performance implications are validated separately under the load-test plan (IB2-64/65).

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Idempotent retries return original requestId
Given a client POSTs with an idempotency-key and receives requestId A
When the same logical request is retried with the same key
Then the API returns the original requestId A and does not re-apply already-processed items (pass if behavior observed)

Scenario: Duplicate IDs within a single POST are reported and not re-enqueued
Given a single POST contains duplicate item IDs
When payload validation runs
Then duplicates are reported in the validation summary (error code) and duplicate items are not enqueued for processing (binary verification)

Scenario: Unit tests cover idempotency and duplicate detection
Given unit test coverage targets
When tests run
Then unit tests for idempotency-key behavior and duplicate detection pass (pass if tests succeed)

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want GET /bulk-apply/{requestId}/status to return per-item statuses and aggregated summaries (functional correctness; performance checks separate)
contents.description:
In order to allow clients to query per-item outcomes and aggregated summaries, As an engineer I want a status endpoint that returns per-item statuses and an aggregated summary. This story verifies functional correctness; performance and SLA validation are covered in load-test stories (IB2-64/65).

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Return final per-item statuses for completed request
Given a completed requestId with per-item outcomes persisted
When GET /bulk-apply/{requestId}/status is called
Then the API returns per-item statuses { itemId, outcome: success|failure|retryable_error }, a top-level aggregated summary, and final=true (pass if response matches persisted data)

Scenario: Return current statuses for in-progress request
Given a requestId still processing
When status is queried
Then the API returns current per-item statuses and final=false (binary verification)

Scenario: Integration tests for status endpoint
Given completed and in-progress requests in a test DB
When integration tests run
Then tests validate correct responses for both states (pass if assertions succeed)

contents.summary: As a platform engineer, I want a single-source-of-truth OpenAPI contract and generated mock fixtures so UI and backend avoid integration drift (performance SLA validation excluded)
contents.description:
In order to ensure consistent contract consumption between UI and backend, As a platform engineer I want an OpenAPI spec for bulk-apply and validation endpoints checked into the repo and used to generate mock fixtures and client SDKs. This story focuses on contract parity and excludes performance SLA validation (performance validations are handled by IB2-64/65/66).

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: OpenAPI spec authored and versioned
Given a repository branch for the spec
When the OpenAPI spec is created and committed
Then the spec is present in the repo and versioned (pass if file exists in repo with version tag)

Scenario: Mock fixtures and client SDK generated
Given the OpenAPI spec in repo
When generation tooling runs
Then mock server fixtures and client SDKs (or example payloads) are produced and published to the shared mocks location (binary verification)

Scenario: Contract compatibility test in CI
Given the generated fixtures and spec
When CI runs the contract compatibility job
Then breaking changes are detected by the test (pass if job runs and reports compatibility status)

contents.summary: As product/engineer, I want a time-boxed discovery spike to define & sign the canonical validation rules so implementation can be estimated
contents.description:
In order to convert validation requirements into estimable implementation work, As a product/engineer I want a 2–3 workday discovery spike that produces a signed canonical validation rule list and maps each rule to an implementation sub-task.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Discovery outputs a signed rules document
Given the discovery spike is executed within the 2–3 workday timebox
When the team presents the rule recommendations to Product and Engineering reviewers
Then a signed rules document (one sentence per rule) is attached to the story and marked approved

Scenario: Each rule mapped to an implementation sub-task
Given the signed rules document is attached
When the team breaks down the rules
Then every rule has a corresponding implementation sub-task created, linked to the story (one sub-task per rule)

Scenario: Timebox enforcement
Given the spike start date is recorded
When 3 workdays pass
Then the spike is closed and a clear 'outcome' (signed doc or outstanding questions) is recorded (pass if signed doc attached)

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want POST /bulk-apply/validate implemented for the core signed rules so UI and apply-engine can run pre-flight checks
contents.description:
In order to prevent invalid bulk-apply requests from entering the apply-engine, As an engineer I want the validation API implemented for the core rules defined in the signed rule-set produced by IB2-57.

Notes/Constraint: This implementation story is estimable only after the IB2-57 signed rules document is attached to this story. If the signed rules are not attached, the story is Not Ready.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Validate up to 1000 items according to signed rules
Given the signed validation rules document is attached to this story
When a client POSTs <= 1000 items to POST /bulk-apply/validate following the OpenAPI contract
Then the API returns per-item results: { itemId, valid: true|false, errorCode?, errorMessage? } consistent with the signed rules

Scenario: Core rules implemented
Given the signed rules include item-stage-compatibility, required-metadata-presence, and permission-checks
When tests run against each rule
Then unit tests pass for each rule (binary pass/fail) and integration tests exercise the API contract against stubs

Scenario: Not Ready without signed rules
Given the signed rules document is NOT attached
When the team attempts to estimate or implement
Then the story is treated as Not Ready and blocked until IB2-57 sign-off is attached

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want the remaining validation rules implemented and an errorCode->UI mapping so consumers get consistent error semantics
contents.description:
In order to provide consistent, actionable validation feedback to UI consumers, As an engineer I want the remaining validation rules implemented and a canonical errorCode -> UI message mapping derived from the signed rule-set produced by IB2-57.

Notes/Constraint: This work is dependent on IB2-57. The story is estimable only after the signed rule-set is attached.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Implement staged-request-constraints and idempotency/duplicate rules
Given the signed rules document is attached
When the validation service processes payloads exercising staged-request-constraints and duplicate/idempotency cases
Then per-item responses reflect the correct valid/invalid flag and proper errorCode values as defined in the signed rules

Scenario: Error code -> UI message mapping file
Given the signed rules and example fixtures
When the mapping file is produced
Then a mapping file (errorCode -> exact UI message) is attached to the story and validated against sample fixtures (pass if mapping reproduces messages for all sample cases)

Scenario: Contract tests
Given the OpenAPI spec and generated fixtures
When contract tests run in CI for the validation API
Then tests pass against the example fixtures and the OpenAPI spec (binary pass/fail)

contents.summary: As a product engineer, I want a time-boxed discovery spike to define canonical outcome-determination rules and sample datasets so apply logic can be scoped
contents.description:
In order to produce deterministic, testable outcome behavior for the apply engine, As a product engineer I want a 2–3 workday discovery spike that defines and signs canonical outcome rules and produces sample datasets for each canonical scenario (staged, multi-stage, standalone, already-complete, conflict scenarios).

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Signed canonical outcome rules
Given the discovery spike is executed within the timebox
When stakeholders (Product + Engineering) review results
Then a signed outcome-rules document is attached to the story covering all required scenarios (pass if signed doc attached)

Scenario: Sample datasets provided
Given the signed rules document
When sample datasets are created
Then representative sample datasets illustrating each canonical scenario are attached to the story

Scenario: Implementation sub-tasks created
Given the signed rules document is attached
When the team decomposes the rules
Then implementation sub-tasks are created for each major behavior and linked to the story

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want the core outcome-determination engine to apply stage transitions and emit audit events according to the signed rules
contents.description:
In order to reliably advance items through workflow stages and maintain auditability, As an engineer I want the core outcome-determination logic implemented to apply stage transitions and emit audit events per-item, driven by the signed outcome rules produced by IB2-60.

Notes/Constraint: This story is estimable only after IB2-60's signed rules and sample datasets are attached. Implementation must match the signed rules exactly.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Apply stage transitions for eligible items
Given the signed rules document and sample dataset for 'all items eligible'
When the outcome module runs against the sample dataset
Then each eligible item has the expected stage transition applied and a per-item change record persisted

Scenario: Emit audit events
Given the same run
When transitions are applied
Then audit events are emitted for each item change and persisted (pass if events persisted for all changed items)

Scenario: Unit/integration tests validate canonical scenarios
Given sample datasets from IB2-60
When unit and integration tests run
Then tests covering the canonical scenarios pass (binary pass/fail)

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want deterministic conflict handling, locking, and conflict codes per the signed outcome rules
contents.description:
In order to ensure deterministic outcomes for concurrent apply attempts, As an engineer I want conflict resolution and locking semantics implemented in accordance with the signed outcome rules from IB2-60 so concurrent attempts produce deterministic success/failure outcomes and clear conflict codes.

Notes/Constraint: This story requires the IB2-60 signed rules document (locking strategy and conflict semantics). It is Not Ready until that document is attached.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Deterministic resolution for concurrent conflicting attempts
Given the signed rules and the conflict scenario sample dataset
When two concurrent apply attempts targeting the same items are executed
Then one attempt succeeds and the other returns a deterministic conflict outcome code as specified in the signed rules

Scenario: Tests validate locking & audit events
Given the locking strategy in the signed rules
When automated tests run concurrent conflict scenarios
Then tests assert deterministic outcomes and emitted audit events match the signed rules (pass if assertions succeed)

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want the outcome module behind a feature flag with compatibility tests comparing new behavior to prior outputs
contents.description:
In order to safely roll out the new outcome module and validate backward-compatibility, As an engineer I want the outcome module implemented behind a feature flag and a suite of compatibility tests that compare the flagged behavior against prior Grouped Review outputs using the signed rules and sample datasets from IB2-60.

Notes/Constraint: This story is dependent on IB2-60 sign-off; it is Not Ready until the signed outcome rules and sample datasets are attached.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Feature flag deployment
Given the outcome module implementation
When the module is deployed behind a feature flag
Then it can be toggled on/off and does not affect non-flagged traffic (binary verification)

Scenario: Compatibility tests pass against canonical scenarios
Given sample datasets and prior Grouped Review outputs
When compatibility tests run with the feature flag enabled
Then flagged behavior matches previous outputs for canonical scenarios or documented diffs are approved by Product (pass if tests pass or diffs approved)

Scenario: End-to-end tests run
Given the sample datasets
When E2E tests execute against the feature-flagged deployment
Then tests pass for canonical datasets (binary pass/fail)

contents.summary: As SRE/tester, I want a short discovery spike to define concurrency targets and a signed test-plan so load tests are scoped correctly
contents.description:
In order to run meaningful load tests and measure against realistic SLAs, As SRE/tester I want a 2-workday discovery spike that agrees on concurrency targets, measurement criteria (95th/99th), and produces a signed test-plan to be used by load-test implementation stories.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Signed test-plan produced
Given the 2-workday timebox is observed
When SRE and product/engineering agree on targets
Then a signed test-plan with concurrency targets and measurement criteria (95th/99th thresholds) is attached to the story (pass if attached)

Scenario: Test-plan used to scope follow-ups
Given the signed test-plan
When downstream load-test stories are estimated
Then estimates reference the signed concurrency targets and measurement criteria (binary verification during estimation)

contents.summary: As an SRE, I want a load-test harness and fixtures that simulate 1000-item batches according to the signed test-plan
contents.description:
In order to measure apply-engine performance under target concurrency, As an SRE I want a load-test harness and fixtures that simulate up to 1000-item batches and follow the signed test-plan produced by IB2-64.

Notes/Constraint: This story is estimable only after IB2-64's signed test-plan is attached. The harness implementation verifies functional and performance metrics as defined by the test-plan.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Harness simulates target batches and concurrency
Given the signed test-plan is attached
When the harness runs with configured concurrency from the test-plan
Then it can simulate bulk apply batches up to 1000 items and collect required metrics (95th/99th response times, queue latencies, CPU/memory, DB IO) and export results

Scenario: End-to-end run exercises apply-engine instance
Given the harness and fixtures
When an end-to-end run is executed (with validation stubs or real validation per test-plan)
Then the run completes and produces an exportable results set for dashboarding (binary pass/fail)

contents.summary: As an SRE, I want CI integration and dashboarding for load-test runs so historical performance is tracked
contents.description:
In order to detect regressions and track performance trends, As an SRE I want load-test runs integrated into CI and results persisted to dashboards with historical charts for 95th/99th latencies and resource metrics.

Notes/Constraint: This work depends on the signed test-plan from IB2-64 for pass/fail thresholds. CI integration of the harness (IB2-65) can be implemented functionally prior to test-plan thresholds, but threshold-based pass/fail requires IB2-64.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: CI can trigger load-test runs and persist results
Given a configured CI job and the load-test harness
When a scheduled or on-demand run executes
Then results are persisted to the performance dashboard and are queryable (binary pass/fail)

Scenario: Historical trend charts available
Given persisted results over multiple runs
When the dashboard is viewed
Then historical trend charts exist for 95th/99th latencies and resource metrics (pass if charts render with historical data)

Scenario: Pass/fail automation against test-plan thresholds
Given the signed test-plan is attached
When runs complete
Then CI automation marks pass/fail against thresholds defined in the test-plan (assertion only valid after IB2-64 sign-off)

contents.summary: As an SRE, I want a prioritized remediation task list created from load-test findings so engineering can fix performance regressions
contents.description:
In order to address performance regressions found during load tests, As an SRE I want a concrete remediation task list (pagination/streaming/backpressure recommendations) prioritized with owners and linked to failing test results.

Notes/Constraint: This story is triggered when a load-test run misses thresholds defined in the signed test-plan from IB2-64. Creating the remediation list requires the test-run results attached.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Remediation list created from failing run
Given a load-test run that misses thresholds and the run results are attached
When the results are analyzed by SRE and Engineering
Then a prioritized remediation task list is created with concrete recommendations and owners, and the remediation story(ies) are created and linked to the performance run (pass if tasks created and linked)

Scenario: Follow-up stories created and triaged
Given remediation tasks are created
When Product/Engineering triage backlog
Then remediation stories exist in the backlog with owners and estimated size (binary verification)

contents.summary: Bulk Apply UI — Build UI against contract-derived mock fixtures
contents.description:
As a reviewer I want the Bulk Apply UI built against the canonical OpenAPI-derived mock fixtures so QA can validate UI behaviors up to 1,000 items without depending on backend readiness.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Given the OpenAPI-generated mock fixtures
  When the Bulk Apply page loads
  Then the UI presents the same Apply button label behavior, selection, tooltips, snackbars, and visual states as Grouped Review using the contract-derived mock responses (supports up to 1000 items)
- The Apply button label displays "Apply (N)" for N between 1 and 1000 and updates with selection changes
- UI uses the shared mock server/fixture from the API Contract story (not ad-hoc fixtures)
- Unit tests cover button state logic and tooltip text

contents.summary: Bulk Apply UI — Accessibility, snackbars, and integration tests
contents.description:
As a reviewer I want accessibility checks, tooltip & snackbar parity, and integration tests so the UI meets UX and a11y requirements.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tooltip strings match UI copy exactly for enabled/disabled states
- Snackbars show success or failure per mocked per-item outcomes and are dismissible with matching tone/duration as Grouped Review
- Automated accessibility checks (WCAG contrast, keyboard-focus) pass for Apply button and controls
- Integration test verifies full UI flow against the contract-derived mock (select -> apply -> observe outcomes)

contents.summary: Bulk Apply UI — Integration wiring & feature-flag to real API
contents.description:
As an engineer I want a non-blocking task to wire the UI to real endpoints behind a feature flag so we can switch from mocks to the live apply-engine when available.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Feature-flagged wiring exists to swap mock endpoints for real API endpoints without UI changes
- A small smoke integration test validates the toggle (when flag on, UI calls real endpoints in a test environment)
- The wiring task is explicitly marked non-blocking for the UI story and is tracked separately

contents.summary: As a product owner, I want permission migration and audit logging for promoted pages so security and traceability are preserved
contents.description:
As a product owner / I want permissions to be migrated or re-evaluated when a sub-page becomes standalone, and an audit log entry created for the operation / So that administrators can verify access and trace changes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Permission inheritance logic for promoted pages is defined and applied (simple inheritance for MVP).
- An audit log entry records the move, actor, timestamp, and affected pages.
- Tests validate permission propagation in common scenarios.

contents.summary: As an Admin, I want Resource Publish Templates to expose Grouped Request notification options when MCP grouped requests are enabled so template configurators can select correct notification behaviour
contents.description:
In order to allow template configurators to control notifications for grouped requests, As an Admin I want the Resource Publish Templates UI to surface specific, documented Grouped Request notification options when MCP 'Enable Grouped Requests' is ON and the template's User Notifications mode is set to 'Select'.

Acceptance Criteria (binary, Gherkin):

Scenario 1 — Visibility condition
Given MCP 'Enable Grouped Requests' = ON
And a template is configured for Grouped Requests
When an Admin edits the template and User Notifications = 'Select'
Then an additional section 'Grouped Request Notifications' appears under Requester Emails with the defined options

Scenario 2 — Defined notification options exist and persist
Given the additional options are visible
When the Admin selects any combination of those options and saves the template
Then the selected options persist and are applied when grouped requests are created

Scenario 3 — Tenant-level customization documented
Given a tenant has custom notification requirements
When an Admin inspects template behavior
Then documentation indicates inheritance rules and how tenant-level overrides are applied (link to doc in UI)

Scenario 4 — Tests validate visibility and selection
Given automated UI/unit tests run
When the MCP flag and template modes change
Then tests assert the 'Grouped Request Notifications' section visibility and selection persistence

Notes: The implementation scope is limited to surfacing these explicit options and documenting inheritance behavior. Further feature work (e.g., delivery tracking, multilingual templates) will be handled in separate stories.

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want the minimal Bulk Apply API contract and POST /bulk-apply accept endpoint so clients can submit up to 1000 items and receive acceptance acknowledgement (functional behavior only)
contents.description:
In order to accept client bulk-submissions and respond immediately with acceptance acknowledgement, As an engineer I want a minimal OpenAPI contract and a POST /bulk-apply endpoint that enforces payload size limits and returns acceptance. This story focuses on functional correctness; any performance SLAs are validated separately via load-tests from IB2-64/65.

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Accept <= 1000 item IDs
Given a client POSTs <= 1000 item IDs to /bulk-apply following the OpenAPI contract
When the request is validated
Then the API returns 202 Accepted with JSON { requestId, accepted: true } (pass if response schema and status match)

Scenario: Reject > 1000 item IDs
Given a client POSTs > 1000 item IDs
When the request is received
Then the API returns 413 Payload Too Large with a clear error code and message (binary verification)

Scenario: OpenAPI spec & fixture presence
Given the OpenAPI spec is the contract source-of-truth
When the spec is checked into repo
Then example fixture payloads for UI mocks are generated from the spec and available (pass if fixtures present)

contents.summary: As an engineer, I want a Bulk Apply worker and persistence so accepted bulk requests are processed asynchronously (functional acceptance only; perf validation is separate)
contents.description:
In order to process bulk-apply requests asynchronously and persist per-item outcomes, As an engineer I want a queue and worker that stores per-item success/failure outcomes and a processing-complete marker. Performance SLAs are out-of-scope for this story and validated separately with load tests (IB2-64/65).

Acceptance Criteria (Gherkin, binary pass/fail):

Scenario: Worker processes accepted request and persists outcomes
Given a valid accepted requestId in the queue
When the worker processes the batch
Then per-item outcomes (success|failure) are persisted to the DB and a processing-complete marker is written (pass if persisted for all items)

Scenario: Integration test for worker behavior
Given a test DB and a 10-item accepted batch queued
When integration tests run
Then the worker picks up the job and writes per-item results for the 10-item batch (binary pass/fail)
